attributes.title: Web Attacks — Investigate
attributes.visState:
{"title": "Web Attacks — Investigate", "type": "markdown", "aggs": [], "params": {"fontSize": 12, "openLinksInNewTab": true, "markdown": "### 🌐 Web Attacks — investigate
**Open:** [Discover (raw alerts)](/app/discover) · [Wazuh app](/app/wazuh) · [MITRE T1190](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/)

**Which IP is the attacker?** `data.clientip` = **X-Forwarded-For**, the real external client (populated when the request came via your reverse proxy); `data.srcip` = the address the web server logged (the **proxy** when proxied, the client on a direct hit). The *Real client IP* panel uses `data.clientip`; the *Source IP* panel uses `data.srcip`. Authorized scanners (payloads ending in `.nasl`) appear in both.

💡 Make hosts clickable: add a URL field-formatter to `agent.id` (see the recipe README)."}}

attributes.title: Web Attacks
attributes.description: Web attacks by attack type, attacked URL/payload and targeted host, over time. Real client IP uses X-Forwarded-For (data.clientip); Source IP uses data.srcip (proxy/as-logged).
